project.name: wismo-retell-agent
project.description: Next.js prototype for customer Where is my order calls. Retell Conversation Flow owns dialogue routing while authenticated backend functions own customer and order validation, retry limits, TrackingMore lookups, and support-ticket escalation.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Server-rendered admin operations dashboard that enforces the ADMIN role and presents order, voice-call, support-ticket, and TrackingMore failure queues.

nodes.name: AdminPage
nodes.summary: Authenticates an administrator, loads shared dashboard aggregates, and renders operational health metrics and recent exception queues.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Admin-only JSON endpoint exposing the same operational aggregates used by the server-rendered dashboard.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Requires an ADMIN session and returns recent operational dashboard data through the shared API error mapper.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Customer order-detail endpoint that combines the requested order ID with the authenticated customer ID to enforce ownership.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Loads one order and its line items only when it belongs to the authenticated customer, otherwise returning a mapped not-found or authorization error.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Customer order-list endpoint scoped to the authenticated customer, including line items and newest-first ordering.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Returns only orders owned by the authenticated customer with line-item details in reverse chronological order.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Storefront credential login endpoint that validates form input, verifies the password hash, and establishes an eight-hour role-aware JWT cookie.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Validates submitted credentials, compares the stored password hash, creates a signed session token, and redirects users by role.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Logout endpoint that deletes the WISMO session cookie and redirects the browser to the storefront login page.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Clears the signed session cookie and redirects the user to the storefront root.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated Retell Web Call endpoint that verifies optional order ownership, records internal call state, and returns a browser-call access token without adding phone-number or outbound flows.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Creates a customer-owned voice-call record, requests a Retell browser-call token with trusted dynamic variables, and persists success or provider failure state.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Central authentication boundary for issuing signed JWT sessions and resolving current users with role checks against the database.

nodes.name: createSessionToken
nodes.summary: Issues an eight-hour HS256 JWT containing the verified user's identity and role.

nodes.name: requireUser
nodes.summary: Verifies the signed session cookie, reloads the user from the database, and enforces an optional expected role.

nodes.name: dashboard.ts
nodes.summary: Shared operational dashboard query service that concurrently loads order totals and recent voice calls, tickets, and failed tracking lookups.

nodes.name: getDashboardData
nodes.summary: Runs four Prisma queries in parallel to produce the common admin dashboard data shape.

nodes.name: db.ts
nodes.summary: Exports the shared Prisma client and caches it during development hot reloads to avoid exhausting database connections.

nodes.name: trackingmore.ts
nodes.summary: TrackingMore client that fetches live shipment status, normalizes carrier-specific payload variants, and records every success or explicit provider/configuration failure without mock fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Defensive record coercion handles legacy and current TrackingMore response envelope variants while preserving explicit provider failures.

nodes.name: extractSummary
nodes.summary: Normalizes a TrackingMore shipment response into the project's stable tracking summary fields.

nodes.name: getTrackingStatus
nodes.summary: Fetches live shipment data from TrackingMore with cache disabled, logs success or explicit failures, and returns a normalized status without substituting mock data.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated Retell function endpoint that validates call arguments and delegates support-ticket creation to the WISMO backend service.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates a Retell request, parses the escalation reason and call identifier, creates a backend-owned support ticket, and returns a normalized API response.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated Retell function endpoint that validates tracking requests and delegates live shipment lookup to the WISMO backend.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates the Retell request, extracts the call and optional order identifiers, and returns tracking data obtained through the backend tracking workflow.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated Retell function endpoint for paginated recent-order listing within the current voice-call context.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates a Retell request, validates listing navigation arguments, and delegates ownership-scoped order retrieval to the WISMO service.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated Retell function endpoint that resolves a spoken order number against orders owned by the current customer call.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates the Retell request, normalizes validated function arguments, and delegates ownership-safe order resolution to the backend.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Retell webhook endpoint that authenticates event delivery, records call events, and updates persisted voice-call state for terminal call events.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Authenticates and parses a Retell webhook, records the event for a known voice call, and updates call status when the call ends or is analyzed.

nodes.name: http.ts
nodes.summary: Shared HTTP error adapter that converts validation and application failures into consistent JSON responses.

nodes.name: apiError
nodes.summary: Maps Zod validation failures and general exceptions to structured Next.js JSON error responses.

nodes.name: retell.ts
nodes.summary: Retell request boundary utilities for function authentication and extraction of call identifiers and function arguments.

nodes.name: requireRetellFunctionAuth
nodes.summary: Rejects Retell function and webhook requests whose authorization header does not match the configured backend secret.

nodes.name: getRetellCallId
nodes.summary: Extracts and validates the Retell call identifier from supported request payload shapes.

nodes.name: getRetellArgs
nodes.summary: Returns the function argument object from a Retell request payload using the supported envelope shape.

nodes.name: wismo-guardrails.ts
nodes.summary: Pure WISMO policy helpers for order-number normalization, shipment target grouping, and bounded order-list navigation.

nodes.name: normalizeOrderNumber
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes spoken or typed order numbers by trimming, uppercasing, removing separators, and normalizing the order prefix.

nodes.name: trackingTargetsForOrder
nodes.summary: Groups line items by non-empty tracking identifier so one live carrier lookup can serve all items in the same shipment.

nodes.name: nextListingState
nodes.summary: Computes bounded listing pagination state while enforcing the configured repeat limit for voice-order enumeration.

nodes.name: wismo.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated WISMO application service owning customer-call lookup, order ownership checks, listing limits, live TrackingMore access, and support-ticket persistence.

nodes.name: resolveOrder
nodes.summary: Normalizes an order number, verifies it belongs to the customer associated with the active Retell call, and persists the selected order or failed-attempt state.

nodes.name: listRecentOrders
nodes.summary: Lists a bounded page of recent orders for the current customer and persists pagination and repetition state for the voice call.

nodes.name: trackingForCall
nodes.summary: Selects an ownership-verified order, groups its shipments, and obtains each live shipment status exclusively through TrackingMore.

nodes.name: createEscalationTicket
nodes.summary: Creates an idempotent support escalation for the authenticated voice call and marks the call as escalated in one transaction.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Authenticated order-detail page that scopes its database lookup to the current customer, renders persisted order and shipment identifiers, and offers a Retell web-call entry point for that order.

nodes.name: OrderDetailPage
nodes.summary: Requires a customer session, retrieves only the requested customer-owned order with line items, and renders delivery, tracking-identifier, and voice-assistance controls.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Authenticated customer orders page that queries only the signed-in customer's orders, summarizes fulfillment activity, and exposes order-specific navigation plus a general Retell web-call entry point.

nodes.name: OrdersPage
nodes.summary: Requires a customer session, loads that customer's orders in reverse chronological order, and renders summary metrics, totals, and links to order details.

nodes.name: app-header.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared authenticated-workspace header that adjusts its home destination and role label for customer and operations modes while providing logout navigation.

nodes.name: AppHeader
nodes.summary: Renders role-aware product navigation for customer and admin workspaces with a shared logout control.

nodes.name: voice-call-button.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side Retell Web Call control that exchanges authenticated order context for a short-lived access token, manages browser call lifecycle state, and surfaces connection errors.

nodes.name: VoiceCallButton
nodes.summary: Starts and stops a Retell browser call using an access token returned by the authenticated backend, optionally passing order context while keeping media and error state local to the control.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Defines the environment contract for PostgreSQL, JWT authentication, Retell Web Call integration, TrackingMore access, and the public application URL without supplying live credentials.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Records the project's implementation boundaries: Retell Conversation Flow owns visible dialogue while authenticated backend functions own authorization, order ownership, retry controls, TrackingMore access, and ticket writes. It also prohibits mock shipment fallback and non-Web-Call Retell flows.

nodes.name: CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Defines the WISMO domain vocabulary for customers, orders, line items, split shipments, tracking IDs, and customer-safe order notes. The glossary establishes line-item tracking as authoritative for split shipments.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Introduces the Next.js WISMO Retell voice-agent prototype and its split between Conversation Flow dialogue routing and authenticated backend operations. It documents local PostgreSQL setup, Prisma lifecycle commands, demo workspaces, voice-function endpoints, and provider setup constraints.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Provisions the local PostgreSQL 16 service with WISMO development credentials, host port 5430, persistent storage, and a readiness health check.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Defines the private Next.js application package, development and production scripts, Vitest tests, and Prisma generation, migration, and seed workflows. Its dependencies provide React UI, Retell browser calling, JWT and password security, validation, and database access.

nodes.name: tracking_details.txt
nodes.summary: Contains four concrete YunExpress shipment histories from China to Australia, including carrier and last-mile tracking identifiers, customer order numbers, delivery states, and chronological checkpoint events. Three examples are delivered and one remains in transit awaiting collection.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Configures strict, no-emit TypeScript compilation for the Next.js application using ES2022, bundler module resolution, isolated modules, incremental checking, and the @ alias for src files.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Controls project-specific exclusions for Understand Anything scans, including generated analysis artifacts and test-file patterns.

nodes.name: next-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Generated TypeScript declarations that load Next.js framework, image, and route types for the application.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Next.js application configuration and enables React strict mode while leaving a clear extension point for deployment hardening.

nodes.name: 20260619134847_init migration
nodes.summary: Initial PostgreSQL migration that creates the customer, order, Retell call, support-ticket, and TrackingMore lookup-log persistence model with its indexes and relationships.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: Stores authenticated customer and administrator identities used to establish ownership of orders, calls, and support tickets.

nodes.name: Order
nodes.summary: Persists customer-owned orders, fulfillment metadata, shipping identifiers, and delivery estimates used by backend order lookup.

nodes.name: VoiceCall
nodes.summary: Tracks each Retell web-call session, its authenticated customer and optional order, retry counters, listing cursor, and lifecycle status.

nodes.name: VoiceCallEvent
nodes.summary: Stores timestamped structured events emitted during a Retell call for auditing and journey reconstruction.

nodes.name: SupportTicket
nodes.summary: Persists backend-created customer support escalations with optional call linkage and open or resolved status.

nodes.name: TrackingLookupLog
nodes.summary: Audits live TrackingMore lookup attempts by order, recording success, response status, and failure details without substituting mock shipment state.

nodes.name: 20260619190000_add_line_items_and_password_auth migration
nodes.summary: Adds password-hash authentication to users and introduces relational line items with parcel-level carrier and tracking identifiers.

nodes.name: LineItem
nodes.summary: Stores products and parcel-level tracking identifiers beneath an order, supporting normal and split-shipment lookup.

nodes.name: migration_lock.toml
nodes.summary: Locks the Prisma migration history to the PostgreSQL provider so migrations are interpreted against the intended database dialect.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Defines the PostgreSQL domain model for authenticated users, customer-owned orders and line items, Retell call state and retry counters, support tickets, and audited TrackingMore lookups.
nodes.languageNotes: Prisma relations encode cascading ownership cleanup while optional order and call links use SetNull to retain operational history.

nodes.name: seed.ts
nodes.summary: Resets dependent records and seeds password-authenticated demo customers, an operations administrator, and fulfilled Australian orders with relational line items and real tracking identifiers.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Deletes existing WISMO records in dependency order, hashes the shared demo password, and recreates customer, admin, order, and line-item fixtures.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Provides the responsive visual system for WISMO login, customer, operations, call-control, order-status, and tracking views using shared semantic color tokens and layout primitives.
nodes.languageNotes: CSS custom properties centralize shipment, warning, success, and error colors across both customer and operations surfaces.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Defines the shared Next.js document shell and WISMO Operations metadata, applying the global stylesheet to customer and operations routes.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Wraps every route in the application HTML and body shell used by the customer and operations interfaces.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the shared secure storefront login for customers and operations staff, posting credentials to the backend login endpoint and displaying authentication errors.

nodes.name: LoginPage
nodes.summary: Builds the customer and administrator sign-in journey, including error feedback, credential fields, and the backend-owned POST submission to /api/login.

layers.name: User Experience
layers.description: Customer and operations pages, shared presentation components, and browser-side Retell Web Call controls render the WISMO journey without owning shipment truth or backend policy.

layers.name: Retell Integration
layers.description: Authenticated Web Call, function, and webhook adapters connect Retell's visible Conversation Flow to backend-owned WISMO capabilities without introducing phone-number or outbound-call flows.

layers.name: Authenticated Backend Policy
layers.description: Server endpoints and application services enforce authentication, customer order ownership, retry limits, dashboard access, response contracts, and support-ticket writes.

layers.name: TrackingMore Live Status
layers.description: The TrackingMore client is the sole live shipment-status integration and keeps carrier lookup behavior distinct from persisted WISMO order data and mock substitutes.

layers.name: Data and Persistence
layers.description: Prisma schema, migrations, database access, and seed data persist users, owned orders, line items, Retell call state, support tickets, and tracking lookup logs.

layers.name: Project Support
layers.description: Runtime examples, framework and TypeScript configuration, local PostgreSQL infrastructure, architecture guidance, domain documentation, and analysis scope support development and operation of WISMO.

tour.title: What WISMO Is and Where Its Boundaries Live
tour.description: Start with the project overview and its architectural guardrails. The README introduces the Next.js prototype, local setup, demo workspaces, and Retell function surface; AGENTS.md supplies the crucial ownership split: Retell Conversation Flow controls the spoken journey, while authenticated backend code controls identity, order access, retry limits, live tracking, and ticket writes.

tour.title: Application Shell and Secure Landing Page
tour.description: With the system boundaries established, follow the browser into the shared Next.js shell and landing page. RootLayout applies the common document structure and visual system, while LoginPage renders the single customer-and-operations sign-in journey whose credentials are submitted to the backend rather than handled in the UI.
tour.languageLesson: In the Next.js App Router, a root layout wraps every route, while page files define route-specific server or client interfaces. Keeping authentication decisions behind a server endpoint prevents the rendered form from becoming a trust boundary.

tour.title: The Customer Order Portal
tour.description: After login, the customer enters an ownership-scoped order workspace. OrdersPage loads only the signed-in customer's recent orders, OrderDetailPage repeats that ownership condition for one selected order, and the shared header and voice-call control connect ordinary portal navigation to browser-based voice assistance.

tour.title: Authentication, Ownership, and Conversation Guardrails
tour.description: The portal's apparent simplicity rests on backend-owned policy. requireUser verifies the signed session and reloads the account, the WISMO service resolves orders only inside the active customer's call context, and pure guardrail helpers normalize spoken order numbers, group split shipments, and bound repeated order-list requests.

tour.title: Starting a Retell Web Call
tour.description: Building on the authenticated customer context, VoiceCallButton asks the backend for a short-lived browser-call token. The Web Call endpoint verifies any supplied order belongs to that customer, creates persisted call state, and sends trusted dynamic variables to Retell; this is intentionally a web-call flow, with no purchased phone number or outbound calling path.

tour.title: Retell Functions Resolve the Customer's Request
tour.description: Once a call is active, Retell invokes narrow authenticated backend functions instead of reading customer data from a static knowledge base. The resolve-order and list-recent-orders routes validate function arguments, then delegate to WISMO service methods that preserve customer ownership, selected-order state, pagination, and retry limits across the conversation.

tour.title: TrackingMore Is the Only Live Shipment Source
tour.description: After an owned order is selected, the tracking function delegates to trackingForCall, which groups line items by shipment and requests each current status through TrackingMore. The client disables caching, normalizes provider responses, records explicit failures, and never silently replaces missing or failed live data with mock shipment status.

tour.title: Escalation and Call Lifecycle Records
tour.description: When self-service cannot finish the journey, the authenticated support function creates an idempotent backend-owned escalation and marks the call accordingly. Retell webhook events then update the known call's lifecycle and preserve event history, allowing support staff to understand both the escalation and the conversation state that produced it.

tour.title: Prisma Persists the Trust Model
tour.description: The Prisma schema turns the preceding policies into durable relationships: users own orders, orders contain parcel-aware line items, voice calls hold retry and selection state, support tickets capture escalation, and tracking logs audit provider outcomes. The shared Prisma client supplies one database boundary while development hot reloads reuse its connection.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma relations express application ownership in the data model, but route and service queries must still scope every read and write explicitly. Migrations preserve the chronological database contract as authentication and split-shipment support evolve.

tour.title: Operations Observe Failures and Escalations
tour.description: The administrator view closes the operational loop. AdminPage enforces the ADMIN role, then aggregates order activity, voice calls, support tickets, and TrackingMore failures so staff can act on explicit provider errors and escalations without weakening the customer-facing ownership checks described earlier.

tour.title: Running the System Locally and in Production
tour.description: Finish with the operational contract around the application. package.json defines development, build, test, Prisma generation, migration, and seed workflows; docker-compose provisions a health-checked PostgreSQL service; and .env.example names the database, session, Retell Web Call, TrackingMore, and public-URL settings required to connect the pieces safely.
tour.languageLesson: Environment examples should document variable names without containing live secrets. A container health check makes database readiness observable, while package scripts provide repeatable lifecycle commands for development and deployment.
